about_description: Learn how ClassGamify helps teachers create reusable activities, publish assignment links, and review student results.
about_introduction: ClassGamify is a teacher-facing workspace for turning lesson material into game-based activities and worksheet-style assignments. Create reusable questions, pairs, groups, vocabulary, and notes once, publish student links, then review attempts and results.
activity_ai_error_empty_response: Activity draft generation returned an empty response.
activity_ai_error_parse_response: Could not parse JSON from the activity draft response.
activity_ai_fallback_description: Teacher-ready {subject} activity generated from lesson notes.
activity_ai_fallback_fill_blank_prompt: Complete the {subject} sentence: The lesson term that {clue} is ___.
activity_ai_fallback_learning_goal: Students can recognize and use key {subject} ideas from the lesson.
activity_ai_fallback_open_box_prompt: Open the box: explain or name the {subject} idea from this lesson.
activity_ai_fallback_question_explanatio: {term} is one of the target items from this lesson.
activity_ai_fallback_teacher_note_remix: Switch templates after saving to reuse the same content in another game mode.
activity_ai_fallback_teacher_note_review: Review the draft before assigning it to {gradeBand} learners.
activity_ai_notice_completed_draft: Workers AI returned a partial draft, so missing classroom structures were completed locally.
activity_ai_notice_invalid_draft: Workers AI returned an invalid draft, so a local deterministic draft was used.
activity_ai_notice_missing_credentials: Workers AI credentials are not configured, so a local deterministic draft was used.
activity_ai_prompt_intro: Create a reusable ClassGamify classroom activity draft.
activity_ai_prompt_return_shape: Return exactly this JSON object shape:
activity_ai_prompt_rule_answer_in_option: - Every question answer must appear in its options array.
activity_ai_prompt_rule_explanation: - Every question should include a brief teacher-approved explanation.
activity_ai_prompt_rule_reusable_content: - Include enough questions, pairs, and groups for the selected template and for reuse in other templates.
activity_ai_source_max_error: Source notes are too long. Keep them under 2000 characters.
activity_ai_source_min_error: Add a lesson topic, vocabulary list, or source notes.
activity_api_error_create_load_failed: Activity was saved but could not be loaded.
activity_api_error_duplicate_load_failed: Duplicated activity was saved but could not be loaded.
activity_api_error_remix_load_failed: Remixed activity was saved but could not be loaded.
activity_api_error_remix_same_template: Choose a different template to remix into.
activity_created_panel_found_body: Review the structured content, keep building the library, or publish it from the activity card when you are ready to share it with students.
activity_created_panel_loading_body: Loading the newly saved activity and next classroom actions.
activity_created_panel_loading_title: New activity is being added to your library.
activity_created_panel_missing_body: The activity was saved, but it is not visible in the current list view yet. Filters or pagination may be hiding it.
activity_draft_meta_checklist_add_explan: Add short explanations before students see answer feedback.
activity_draft_meta_checklist_add_more_s: Add more structured pairs or groups to unlock more templates.
activity_draft_meta_checklist_add_questi: Add questions before publishing quiz-style work.
activity_draft_meta_checklist_adjust_lev: Adjust wording for your class level.
activity_draft_meta_description: Review the generated content before saving it to the activity library.
activity_draft_meta_provider_description: Generated locally from the source notes, so treat it like a scaffold before assigning.
Generated by Workers AI and converted into the teacher-reviewable activity editor contract.
Drafted by Workers AI, then completed locally where required classroom structures were missing.
activity_edit_access_archived_descriptio: Restore this activity from the archived library before editing its structured content.
activity_edit_access_ready_description: Update reusable activity content before publishing or reusing it across templates.
activity_edit_page_load_error: Activity could not be loaded. Refresh the page or return to the activity library.
activity_editor_default_description: Quick classroom practice that can become a quiz, match game, or worksheet.
activity_editor_default_groups_text:
Fruit | apple, banana
Drink | milk, water
activity_editor_default_learning_goal: Students can recognize key words and connect them with simple meanings.
activity_editor_default_pairs_text:
apple | fruit
milk | drink
rice | grain
activity_editor_default_questions_text:
Which word means a red or green fruit? | apple | apple, bread, water | Apple is the fruit clue.
Which drink is white? | milk | milk, rice, egg | Milk is the white drink.
activity_editor_default_source_summary: Teacher-created activity from a unit vocabulary list.
activity_editor_default_teacher_notes_te:
Use quiz mode for homework.
Use matching pairs as a class warmup.
activity_editor_default_vocabulary_text: apple, bread, milk, rice, water, egg
activity_editor_preview_description: This {template} example is still a draft. Review the structured fields on the left, save it as an activity, then publish from your library to create the student link.
activity_form_ai_source_placeholder: Paste vocabulary, a reading passage, or lesson notes.
activity_form_footer_create_hint: Saved activities appear in the teacher dashboard and can later be published as assignments.
activity_form_footer_edit_hint: Changes update the reusable activity used by future assignments.
activity_form_groups_description: Used by drag classification and category sorting.
activity_form_pairs_description: Used by matching games, line matching, and pair-card templates.
activity_form_questions_description: One question per line. Use vertical bars to separate the prompt, answer, optional choices, and optional explanation. Use / or ; inside the answer field for acceptable alternatives.
activity_form_questions_placeholder: Prompt | answer | option, option, option | explanation
activity_form_sign_in_required_message: You can draft the activity here, but saving requires a teacher account.
activity_form_source_materials_descripti: Link uploaded audio, worksheet images, documents, or spreadsheets to this draft for future AI extraction and editing. Student assignments will not expose these file references.
activity_form_source_materials_empty_att: No classroom files are attached to this activity yet.
activity_form_source_materials_empty_ava: No uploaded classroom files are available yet.
activity_form_source_materials_load_erro: Classroom files could not be loaded.
activity_form_source_materials_sign_in_h: Sign in to attach classroom files to saved activities.
activity_form_toast_draft_generation_fai: Activity draft could not be generated.
activity_form_toast_edit_missing_activit: Activity could not be identified for editing.
activity_form_toast_local_draft_generate: Draft filled from the local generator.
activity_form_toast_missing_draft_source: Add a topic, vocabulary list, or source notes first.
activity_form_toast_sign_in_generate_dra: Sign in to generate AI activity drafts.
activity_form_toast_sign_in_save: Sign in to save activities to your teacher library.
activity_form_toast_source_materials_syn: Attached materials synced to AI source notes.
activity_form_vocabulary_description: Separate terms with commas, semicolons, or new lines.
activity_library_card_restore_required: Restore this activity before publishing, duplicating, or remixing it.
activity_library_created_panel_missing_h: The saved activity may be on another page or hidden by filters. The edit action appears once the activity can be resolved for this teacher.
activity_library_empty_archived_descript: Archived activities will appear here after you move them out of the active library.
activity_library_empty_filtered_descript: Try another title, description, template keyword, or template family from your classroom activity library.
activity_library_empty_saved_description: Create the first reusable classroom activity, then publish it as a student assignment link for your class.
activity_library_filter_placeholder: Search by title, description, or template
activity_library_hero_description: The activity model separates questions, pairs, groups, vocabulary, and teacher notes. Template switching and AI creation can both build on this shared contract.
activity_library_load_error: Activities could not be loaded. Refresh the page or sign in again.
activity_library_page_description: Reusable teacher-owned activities. Each activity stores template-neutral content so it can render as different classroom games.
activity_lifecycle_derivation_blocked: Restore this activity before publishing, duplicating, or remixing it.
activity_lifecycle_restore_blocked: Only archived activities can be restored.
activity_preview_default_panel_descripti: The same activity can become class play, homework, or a printable follow-up once the template runner is filled in.
activity_runner_line_match_help: Select a prompt on the left, then select its match on the right.
activity_runner_listening_help: Use the play button, then answer what you heard.
activity_scaffold_fill_blank_description: Worksheet-style sentence completion for target food and drink words.
activity_scaffold_fill_blank_learning_go: Students can complete short sentences with the correct target vocabulary.
activity_scaffold_fill_blank_questions_t:
I drink ___ in the morning. | milk | milk, water, rice, apple | Milk is the drink that fits the sentence.
She eats an ___ after lunch. | apple | apple, bread, water, cup | Apple fits after "an" and is a food.
We have ___ for dinner. | rice | rice, milk, egg, water | Rice is a common dinner food.
Please put the soup in a ___. | bowl | bowl, cup, bread, water | A bowl can hold soup.
activity_scaffold_fill_blank_source_summ: Fill-in example from a food and drink vocabulary lesson.
activity_scaffold_fill_blank_teacher_not:
Use the word bank for starter classes and remove it for challenge mode.
Ask students to read each completed sentence aloud before reviewing scores.
activity_scaffold_food_base_description: Reusable food and drink vocabulary practice for game or worksheet modes.
activity_scaffold_food_base_groups_text:
Food | apple, bread, rice, egg
Drink | milk, water
Container | bowl, cup
activity_scaffold_food_base_learning_goa: Students can recognize everyday food and drink words and connect them to simple meanings.
activity_scaffold_food_base_pairs_text:
apple | fruit
bread | bakery food
milk | drink
rice | grain
water | drink
egg | protein food
bowl | container
cup | container
activity_scaffold_food_base_questions_te:
Which word means a red or green fruit? | apple | apple, bread, water, cup | Apple is the fruit clue.
Which drink is white? | milk | milk, rice, egg, bowl | Milk is the white drink.
Which food is often eaten from a bowl? | rice | rice, water, apple, cup | Rice is commonly served in a bowl.
Which item can hold a drink? | cup | cup, bread, egg, apple | A cup can hold a drink.
activity_scaffold_food_base_source_summa: Example from a short food and drink vocabulary lesson that can become multiple templates.
activity_scaffold_food_base_teacher_note:
Start with the primary template, then check ready modes before publishing.
Use result analysis to choose one reteach activity for the next lesson.
activity_scaffold_food_base_vocabulary_t: apple, bread, milk, rice, water, egg, bowl, cup
activity_scaffold_group_sort_description: Drag-and-sort classification practice with learner-friendly categories.
activity_scaffold_group_sort_learning_go: Students can sort everyday words into food, drink, and container groups.
activity_scaffold_group_sort_source_summ: Sorting example from a food and drink classification task.
activity_scaffold_group_sort_teacher_not:
Review one category at a time before students submit.
Ask students to explain one item choice, then remix the same content as a quiz.
activity_scaffold_line_match_description: Worksheet-style line matching for words, meanings, and categories.
activity_scaffold_line_match_learning_go: Students can connect each food word to the correct meaning or category.
activity_scaffold_line_match_source_summ: Line matching example from a vocabulary matching task.
activity_scaffold_line_match_teacher_not:
Ask students to draw or tap one connection at a time.
Review mismatched lines, then reuse the same pairs as matching cards.
activity_scaffold_listening_description: Short listening checks with spoken prompts and quick answer choices.
activity_scaffold_listening_learning_goa: Students can understand short spoken food sentences and identify the key word.
activity_scaffold_listening_questions_te:
I drink milk in the morning. | milk | milk, rice, apple, bowl | The spoken sentence says milk.
She eats an apple after lunch. | apple | apple, bread, water, cup | The key word in the sentence is apple.
We have rice for dinner. | rice | rice, milk, egg, water | Rice is the food named in the sentence.
The cup has water in it. | water | water, bread, bowl, apple | The spoken sentence says water.
activity_scaffold_listening_source_summa: Listening example from short vocabulary sentences.
activity_scaffold_listening_teacher_note:
Ask students to play each track before reading any transcript.
Use answer reveal after submission so the spoken sentence becomes the review text.
activity_scaffold_match_up_description: Match terms with meanings for quick vocabulary practice and homework.
activity_scaffold_match_up_learning_goal: Students can connect each key word with its meaning or category.
activity_scaffold_match_up_source_summar: Matching game example from a unit vocabulary list.
activity_scaffold_match_up_teacher_notes:
Use shuffled items for independent homework.
Discuss incorrect pairs during review, then remix as line match for worksheet follow-up.
activity_scaffold_matching_pairs_descrip: Memory-style pair review for a class warmup, center, or homework link.
activity_scaffold_matching_pairs_learnin: Students can recall matching word pairs through repeated practice.
activity_scaffold_matching_pairs_source_: Pair matching example from a recall activity.
activity_scaffold_matching_pairs_teacher:
Use this as a low-stakes warmup before a quiz.
Ask pairs to say one sentence after finishing, then check the result summary for reteach words.
activity_scaffold_open_box_description: Prompt reveal activity for speaking, whole-class review, or exit tickets.
activity_scaffold_open_box_learning_goal: Students can answer short prompts using the target vocabulary.
activity_scaffold_open_box_questions_tex:
Name one fruit you like. | apple | | Apple is an example fruit answer.
What do you drink in the morning? | milk | | Milk is a model answer for this prompt.
Say a sentence with rice. | rice | | The answer should use rice in a sentence.
What can hold water? | cup | | Cup is a model answer for this prompt.
activity_scaffold_open_box_source_summar: Box reveal example from speaking prompts.
activity_scaffold_open_box_teacher_notes:
Use whole-class mode and let students answer orally before typing.
Accept close variants when reviewing and reuse strong prompts as fill blanks.
activity_scaffold_quiz_description: Multiple-choice check for fast homework scoring and reteach planning.
activity_scaffold_quiz_learning_goal: Students can choose the correct word from simple multiple-choice prompts.
activity_scaffold_quiz_source_summary: Quiz example from a unit vocabulary list.
activity_scaffold_quiz_teacher_notes_tex:
Publish as homework with answer reveal on.
Use result analysis to choose reteach words, then remix the same content into matching practice.
activity_starter_food_description: A starter English vocabulary activity that can render as quiz, match, lines, or pairs.
activity_starter_food_learning_goal: Students can recognize core food words and connect them to simple meanings.
activity_starter_food_question_apple_exp: Apple is the fruit clue in this vocabulary set.
activity_starter_food_question_apple_pro: Which word means a red or green fruit?
activity_starter_food_question_milk_expl: Milk is the white drink among the answer choices.
activity_starter_food_question_rice_expl: Rice is commonly served in a bowl, unlike the drink choices.
activity_starter_food_question_rice_prom: Which food is often eaten from a bowl?
activity_starter_food_source_summary: Unit vocabulary list about common food and drinks.
activity_starter_food_teacher_note_1: Use the same content as a quiz for homework, line match for worksheet practice, or matching pairs for class warmup.
activity_starter_food_teacher_note_2: Ask students to say one sentence after each correct answer.
activity_starter_science_description: A simple science classification activity for solids, liquids, and gases.
activity_starter_science_learning_goal: Students can classify everyday materials by observable state.
activity_starter_science_question_steam_: Steam is water vapor, so it belongs with gases.
activity_starter_science_source_summary: Lesson notes about states of matter.
activity_starter_science_teacher_note_1: Use group sort in class, then publish the quiz version as homework.
activity_template_draft_guidance_fill_bl: Write each question prompt as a worksheet sentence with ___ or [blank] where the answer belongs.
activity_template_draft_guidance_group_s: Make groups the primary structure; each group needs a clear category label and concrete sortable items.
activity_template_draft_guidance_line_ma: Make pairs the primary structure; each left side should connect cleanly to one right-side answer for line matching.
activity_template_draft_guidance_listeni: Write each question prompt as a short sentence that can be spoken aloud; the answer should be the key word or phrase students identify.
activity_template_draft_guidance_match_u: Make pairs the primary structure; use terms, definitions, translations, examples, or cause-and-effect matches.
activity_template_draft_guidance_matchin: Make pairs the primary structure; keep both sides short enough for memory-style cards.
activity_template_draft_guidance_open_bo: Write each question prompt as a reveal-card speaking or review prompt with a concise model answer.
activity_template_draft_guidance_quiz: Write each question as a concise multiple-choice check with plausible distractors.
activity_template_fill_blank_best_for: Sentence patterns, grammar practice, listening follow-up
activity_template_fill_blank_description: Sentences with missing words, useful for grammar and spelling practice.
activity_template_group_sort_best_for: Grammar families, science groups, topic classification
activity_template_group_sort_description: Students drag items into teacher-defined groups and compare patterns.
activity_template_line_match_best_for: Vocabulary translations, terms and definitions, cause and effect
activity_template_line_match_description: Students connect prompts to matching answers in a worksheet-style flow.
activity_template_listening_description: Students listen to a prompt or sentence, then answer from memory.
activity_template_match_up_best_for: Words and meanings, examples and categories, cause and effect
activity_template_match_up_description: Learners connect terms, definitions, translations, images, or examples.
activity_template_matching_pairs_descrip: A memory-style activity where students reveal and match related cards.
activity_template_open_box_best_for: Speaking prompts, review rounds, classroom participation
activity_template_open_box_description: Students reveal prompts one at a time for whole-class or group play.
activity_template_quiz_best_for: Vocabulary checks, reading comprehension, concept review
activity_template_quiz_description: Multiple-choice questions with immediate scoring for quick checks.
activity_template_readiness_panel_descri: The same structured content can become multiple Wordwall-style activity formats after saving.
activity_template_readiness_panel_empty: Add questions, pairs, or groups to unlock playable templates.
activity_validation_default_source_summa: Teacher-created activity from structured editor input.
activity_validation_error_group_line: Group line {lineNumber} needs "group | item one, item two".
admin_users_ban_reason_placeholder: Enter reason for banning this user…
assignment_api_error_assignment_not_publ: This assignment has not been published for students yet.
assignment_api_error_attempt_limit_reach: This assignment has reached its attempt limit.
assignment_api_error_close_time_future: Choose a future close time for this assignment.
assignment_api_error_create_load_failed: Assignment was saved but could not be loaded.
assignment_api_error_status_load_failed: Assignment status was saved but could not be loaded.
assignment_api_error_student_name_requir: Student name is required for this assignment.
assignment_attempt_answers_error_duplica: Submitted answers include a duplicate item.
Assignment runtime items include a duplicate item id.
assignment_attempt_answers_error_too_man: Submitted answers exceed assignment item count.
assignment_attempt_answers_error_unknown: Submitted answers include an unknown item.
assignment_list_empty_filtered_descripti: Try another assignment title, share id, activity name, or status.
assignment_list_empty_library_descriptio: Open the activity library and publish a saved activity to create a student share link.
assignment_list_page_description: Published activity instances with share links, classroom settings, and result metrics.
assignment_list_page_load_error: Assignments could not be loaded. Refresh the page or sign in again.
assignment_list_published_panel_missing_: The new assignment may be on another page after filtering. The share link actions still use the published link id.
assignment_list_search_placeholder: Search by assignment, activity, or share id
assignment_printable_answer_key_descript: Teacher-only answers from the frozen assignment snapshot.
assignment_printable_empty_description: Add questions, pairs, or groups to the activity before printing this assignment.
assignment_printable_instructions_fallba: Complete the worksheet, then use the student link if your teacher asks for an online submission.
assignment_printable_load_error: Printable worksheet could not be loaded. Return to results and try again.
assignment_printable_page_description: A print-friendly worksheet generated from the frozen assignment snapshot.
assignment_printable_seo_description: Teacher-only printable worksheet generated from a ClassGamify assignment snapshot.
assignment_publish_dialog_close_after_he: Optional. Leave blank to keep the link open until it is closed manually.
assignment_publish_dialog_description: Freeze this activity into a student share link with classroom delivery settings.
assignment_publish_dialog_max_attempts_h: Leave blank for unlimited attempts. Use 1-10 to cap each student identity.
assignment_publish_dialog_time_limit_hel: Optional classroom timer in minutes. Leave blank for no time limit.
assignment_publish_toggle_collect_name_d: Ask learners to type their name before submitting.
assignment_publish_toggle_show_answers_d: Reveal correct answers after an attempt is submitted.
assignment_publish_toggle_shuffle_items_: Prepare this assignment for randomized item order.
assignment_publish_validation_close_time: Close time must be in the future.
assignment_publish_validation_instructio: Instructions must be {max} characters or fewer.
assignment_publish_validation_max_attemp: Max attempts must be a whole number from 1 to 10.
assignment_publish_validation_time_limit: Time limit must be a whole number from 1 to 180 minutes.
assignment_publish_validation_title_max: Assignment title must be {max} characters or fewer.
assignment_publish_validation_title_min: Assignment title must be at least {min} characters.
assignment_publish_validation_title_requ: Add an assignment title before publishing.
assignment_published_panel_found_body: Copy the student link for your class, open the student preview, or jump into the results page before submissions arrive.
assignment_published_panel_loading_body: Loading the newly published assignment link and classroom actions.
assignment_published_panel_loading_title: Student share link is being prepared.
assignment_published_panel_missing_body: Copy the student link for your class or open the student preview. Results will appear once the assignment is visible in this list.
assignment_result_action_copy_brief_fail: Classroom brief could not be copied.
assignment_result_action_copy_follow_up_: Student follow-up could not be copied.
assignment_result_action_copy_item_revie: Item review could not be copied.
assignment_result_action_copy_reteach_pl: Reteach plan could not be copied.
assignment_result_action_export_csv_fail: Results CSV could not be downloaded.
assignment_result_action_gate_add_items_: Add assignment items before copying item review.
assignment_result_action_gate_submit_att: Submit at least one attempt before copying a brief.
Submit at least one attempt before exporting results.
Submit at least one attempt before copying follow-up.
Submit at least one attempt before copying a reteach plan.
assignment_result_empty_attempt_review_d: Completed submissions will show item-level answer details here.
assignment_result_empty_attempt_rows_des: Share the student link, then completed submissions will appear here.
assignment_result_empty_needs_review_des: Every shown submission is currently correct for this assignment snapshot.
assignment_result_empty_search_answer_re: Clear the search or try another student name from this assignment.
assignment_result_empty_search_attempts_: Clear the search or try another student name from this assignment.
assignment_result_empty_search_students_: Clear the search or try another student name from this assignment.
assignment_result_empty_student_summary_: Student summaries appear after at least one submitted attempt.
assignment_result_page_description: Review student attempts, scores, and assignment-level result metrics.
assignment_result_page_load_error: Assignment results could not be loaded. Refresh the page or return to assignments.
assignment_result_page_student_link_clos: This assignment is closed. Reopen it before sharing the student link.
assignment_result_page_student_link_draf: Publish this assignment before sharing a student link.
assignment_result_page_student_link_expi: This assignment link has expired. Students cannot open it from the results page.
assignment_result_section_answer_review_: Item-level answers are scored from the frozen assignment snapshot, so teacher edits never change historical results.
assignment_result_section_classroom_brie: A compact class-ready summary built from the frozen assignment snapshot and submitted attempts.
assignment_result_section_item_performan: Review every prompt from the frozen assignment snapshot, including submitted counts, correct rates, and answer notes.
assignment_result_section_reteach_priori: Items are sorted by the lowest correct rate so teachers can quickly decide what to review with the class.
Submit at least one answered attempt to calculate item review priorities.
assignment_result_section_student_attemp: Latest submitted attempts are shown first, with detailed answer review below.
assignment_result_section_student_summar: Sort students by review priority, best score, name, or attempt volume before reading every submitted answer.
assignment_share_link_copy_failure: Student link could not be copied.
assignment_status_action_failure: Assignment status could not be updated.
assignment_status_error_close_only_publi: Only published assignment links can be closed.
assignment_status_error_reopen_only_clos: Only closed assignment links can be reopened.

contact_checklist_description: A little context helps us answer faster.
contact_checklist_device: Tell us which device, browser, or printer setup you used.
contact_checklist_goal: Say whether this is for a class, tutoring group, family practice, or worksheet follow-up.
contact_checklist_page: Share the activity, template, assignment, page URL, or workflow you mean.
contact_classroom_checklist_description: The best notes are specific enough for us to suggest a workflow, not just answer a support ticket.
contact_classroom_checklist_learners: Share learner count, age range, and whether this is for parents, tutoring, or a classroom.
contact_classroom_checklist_routine: Tell us your subject, weekly rhythm, how students currently receive activities, and which templates matter first.
contact_classroom_checklist_worksheets: Mention the assignment, printable, fill-in, listening, or homework handoff you need first.
contact_classroom_description: Tell us how you want to use ClassGamify with students, tutoring groups, or a school team, and we will help shape the workflow around your routine.
contact_classroom_details_description: These details are added to the email so we can tell whether you need template activities, assignment links, student results, or AI-assisted prep.
contact_classroom_material_placeholder: e.g. English vocab, science groups, math concepts, existing worksheet
contact_classroom_message_template:
Learners:
Class or grade:
Activity material:
Weekly routine:
Template / result need:
Timeline:
contact_classroom_need_placeholder: e.g. word matching, drag sorting, fill blanks, result export
contact_classroom_panel_description: ClassGamify is being built around reusable activities, game templates, assignment links, student attempts, and teacher result views.
contact_classroom_panel_materials_descri: We can prioritize printable packs, review queues, and assignment wording around your class routine.
contact_classroom_panel_rollout_descript: Tell us when you want to try it, and we can point you to the most stable flow first.
contact_classroom_panel_students_descrip: Age, level, group size, and parent or teacher role help us recommend the right workflow.
contact_classroom_placeholder_message: Share learner count, class or grade, activity material, weekly routine, template or worksheet needs, result-review needs, and when you want to try ClassGamify.
contact_classroom_routine_placeholder: e.g. 2 classes per week, one homework link after class
contact_description: Reach the ClassGamify team for product feedback, classroom use, account support, or partnership questions.
contact_email_invalid: Please enter a valid email address
contact_email_support_description: Send product feedback, support questions, or partnership ideas.
contact_message_min: Message must be at least 10 characters
contact_name_min: Name must be at least 3 characters
contact_topic_classroom_description: Requests for classroom sharing, assignment routines, or student-friendly practice.
contact_topic_learning_description: Questions about templates, activity creation, assignment links, or results.
contact_topic_partnership_description: Questions about plans, early access, or collaborations.
create_page_description: Start with structured classroom content: questions, match pairs, categories, vocabulary, learning goal, and teacher notes. The same saved activity can later become a quiz, match game, group sort, worksheet, or assignment.
create_page_input_shape_groups: 3. Groups: label | item one, item two.
create_page_input_shape_notes: 4. Notes and vocabulary as simple lists.
create_page_seo_description: Create a structured classroom activity that can render as game templates and publish to students.
create_page_title: Create once, teach through many templates.
dashboard_overview_action_activities_des: Review your activity library and the structured content each template consumes.
dashboard_overview_action_assignments_de: Track published share links, assignment settings, completions, and average scores.
dashboard_overview_action_student_previe: Open a playable student assignment route with progress, timing, scoring, and review behavior.
dashboard_overview_description: Manage reusable activities, publish classroom assignments, and track student attempts from one workspace.
dashboard_overview_hero_description: ClassGamify separates reusable teacher activities from published assignments and student attempts. Create, publish, play, and review now share one activity data contract that AI drafting and template remixing can build on.
dashboard_overview_hero_title: Activity content is now the center of the product.
dashboard_overview_metric_templates_desc: Template families represented by your active activities
footer_cta_description: Create reusable activities, publish student links, and keep results attached to each assignment run.
footer_cta_title: Turn one lesson into a playable classroom activity
footer_link_articles_desc: Read short guides for templates, assignments, and classroom review.
footer_link_assignments_desc: Track share links, completions, and scores.
footer_link_create_desc: Draft structured content for reusable activities.
footer_link_play_desc: Preview the public student activity route.
footer_link_pricing_desc: Compare teacher and early supporter plans.
footer_link_review_queue_desc: Return to completions, scores, and reteach signals.
footer_link_support_desc: Ask about product, classroom use, or accounts.
footer_link_teachers_desc: Plan activities for classes, tutoring, or teams.
footer_link_templates_desc: Browse the first game-based activity families.
footer_link_worksheets_desc: Start fill-in, line matching, listening, and sorting practice.
footer_tagline: Game-based classroom activities, assignment links, and student results for teachers.
home_call_to_action_description: Create a reusable classroom activity, publish a student link, then review scores and reteach priorities.
home_call_to_action_title: Turn lesson material into playable assignments
home_faqs_items_item_1_answer: Yes. You can browse starter templates, create sample activities, and open student previews before choosing a paid plan.
home_faqs_items_item_2_answer: Paid plans use hosted checkout after sign-in. Available payment methods depend on the payment processor and your location.
home_faqs_items_item_3_answer: Use the free loop first: create an activity, preview the student runner, and publish a sample assignment before deciding whether Pro workflow tools are useful.
home_faqs_items_item_4_answer: If a payment or subscription issue comes up, contact support with your account email and plan details so we can help.
home_faqs_items_item_5_answer: Use the contact page for product feedback, worksheet modes, classroom workflows, assignment links, or result-review questions.
home_features_description: Core tools for turning lesson material into a repeatable classroom activity workflow.
home_features_items_item_1_description: Build reusable questions, pairs, groups, vocabulary, learning goals, and teacher notes in one structured editor.
home_features_items_item_2_description: Render the same content as quiz, matching games, line matching, group sorting, fill-in, listening, pair matching, or box reveals.
home_features_items_item_3_description: Publish student links with attempt limits, timers, answer reveal, shuffle, and close-time settings.
home_features_items_item_4_description: Review scores, item performance, accepted answers, reteach priorities, and student follow-up summaries.
home_features2_description: Built for reusable activity libraries, homework handoff, worksheet-style practice, and results review.
home_hero_description: Create game-based classroom activities, publish assignment links, and review student attempts from the same structured content.
home_hero_title: Make classroom practice feel like a game
home_integration_description: Use ClassGamify alongside the tools teachers already use to hand out work and review progress.
home_integration_items_item_1_descriptio: Share a focused student link before class, tutoring, homework, or review time.
home_integration_items_item_2_descriptio: Create worksheet-style modes for fill-ins, line matching, listening, and drag sorting.
home_integration_items_item_3_descriptio: Keep assignment instructions, delivery rules, and result exports tied to the same frozen snapshot.
home_integration_items_item_4_descriptio: Use the classroom inquiry flow to describe students, routines, worksheets, and result-review needs.
home_integration_items_item_5_descriptio: Use classroom briefs, reteach plans, and follow-up summaries to turn attempts into the next lesson.
home_integration_items_item_6_descriptio: Track roadmap requests for teacher audio, worksheet extraction, AI remixes, and richer templates.
home_integration2_description: Connect activity creation, assignment links, worksheet modes, and result review into one repeatable routine.
home_integration2_title: Bring classroom practice into one loop
home_logo_cloud_title: Built for teachers, tutors, parents, and small learning teams
home_page_seo_description: Create game-based classroom activities, publish student play links, and track assignment results from one teacher workspace.
home_pricing_block_description: Start with the core activity loop, then upgrade when you need larger libraries, AI drafting, richer worksheets, and repeatable assignment workflows.
home_stats_description: A focused launch surface for proving the activity-to-assignment-to-results loop before adding more templates.
home_testimonials_items_item_1_quote: The short loop makes it obvious what to do next: create the activity, share the link, then review who needs help.
home_testimonials_items_item_2_quote: The same content can become a match game, a quiz, or a worksheet-style follow-up without rewriting the lesson.
home_testimonials_items_item_3_quote: The result view turns homework into a quick reteach plan instead of just another score table.
home_testimonials_items_item_4_quote: I can open a student link, watch the attempt summary, and know what to review next.
home_testimonials_items_item_5_quote: The assignment flow is specific enough for a small class: play first, submit next, review after.
home_testimonials_items_item_6_quote: The product is starting with practical templates but clearly leaves room for richer AI-assisted worksheet workflows.
home_testimonials_subtitle: How people use the classroom loop
mail_forgot_password_body: Please click the link below to reset your password.
mail_subscribe_newsletter_body: Thanks for subscribing to ClassGamify updates. We will send product notes about classroom templates, worksheet workflows, AI authoring, and assignment review features.
mail_subscribe_newsletter_subject: Thanks for subscribing to ClassGamify updates
mail_verify_email_body: Please click the link below to verify your email address.

nav_privacy_policy_description: How we protect and handle your data
nav_roadmap_description: See what is live, improving, and planned next.
nav_terms_of_service_description: Legal agreement between you and us
newsletter_description: Get ClassGamify updates about templates, worksheets, AI authoring, and classroom result workflows
newsletter_email_invalid: Please enter a valid email address
newsletter_error: Failed to subscribe to ClassGamify updates
not_found_description: The page you are looking for does not exist
pricing_decision_learners_description: Use ClassGamify when you need to turn lesson content into reusable game-based activities.
pricing_decision_teachers_description: Upgrade when reusable libraries, AI drafting, and classroom result workflows start saving real planning time.
pricing_decision_worksheets_description: Publish separate assignment links so each class run can collect its own completion and score data.
pricing_description: Pricing for creating game-based classroom activities, publishing assignment links, and tracking student results.
pricing_faq_description: Questions about activity templates, assignment links, student results, and school use.
pricing_faq_free_answer: The free plan is for testing the core creation loop: browse templates, create a small number of activities, open student previews, and see whether the workflow fits your class rhythm.
pricing_faq_free_question: What is the free plan for?
pricing_faq_pro_answer: Pro will expand around higher activity and assignment limits, result tracking, AI generation, and faster template remixing.
pricing_faq_schools_answer: That is part of the product direction. School use needs teacher seats, student data rules, permissions, and rollout details, so contact us before a larger deployment.
pricing_faq_schools_question: Can schools or tutoring teams use it?
pricing_faq_student_accounts_answer: The v1 direction is public assignment links with a lightweight student name or anonymous token. Teacher accounts own creation, publishing, and results.
pricing_faq_templates_answer: The first version intentionally starts with a focused set of high-frequency classroom templates so creation, publishing, student play, and results become reliable before the catalog grows.
pricing_faq_templates_question: Why are there only a few templates first?
pricing_plans_free_description: Try the core activity creation loop without a credit card.
pricing_plans_free_features: ["Browse starter templates","Create sample activities","Open student previews","Publish a sample assignment link"]
pricing_plans_lifetime_description: Early access for teachers who want to shape the template and AI roadmap.
pricing_plans_lifetime_features: ["Everything in Teacher Pro","Early access to new templates","Early access to AI remix tools","Founder pricing"]
pricing_plans_pro_description: For teachers who want reusable activities, published assignments, and AI-assisted creation.
pricing_plans_pro_features: ["Larger activity library","More published assignments","Student completion and score tracking","AI-assisted activity drafts","Template remixing workflow"]
pricing_school_description: For multiple teachers, campuses, or long-running cohorts, contact us to plan teacher seats, student data handling, template needs, and rollout timing.
pricing_subtitle: Start with the classroom activity loop, then upgrade for more creation, assignment, AI, and result workflows.
pricing_value_ai_description: Paid plans will expand around AI creation, remixing, more templates, and result reporting.
pricing_value_assignments_description: Save lesson content as reusable activities, then publish separate assignment links for each class run.
pricing_value_templates_description: Start with core templates such as quiz, matching games, line matching, group sorting, fill-ins, listening, pair matching, and box reveal activities.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_0_descriptio: Turn teacher notes, vocabulary lists, and worksheet prompts into reviewable activity drafts without bypassing teacher control.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_1_descriptio: Read teacher-uploaded worksheets into structured prompts, accepted answers, and printable follow-up modes.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_2_descriptio: Add multi-teacher sharing, class-level result retention, and permission rules for schools and learning centers.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_0_description: Teachers can create reusable structured activities, publish assignment links, and keep snapshots stable for student attempts.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_1_description: Quiz, matching games, group sorting, line matching, fill-ins, listening, pair matching, and box reveal modes share one content model.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_0_descri: Make completion, scores, item misses, exports, and review filters clearer for teacher follow-up.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_1_descri: Tighten printable follow-up, fill-in, line matching, and listening flows around the same assignment snapshot.
roadmap_columns_backlog_description: Next product bets once the core classroom loop stays dependable.
roadmap_columns_done_description: The teacher and student foundation that must stay reliable while copied starter surfaces are retired.
roadmap_columns_in_progress_description: Current focus: make results, worksheet delivery, and template polish more classroom-ready.
roadmap_description: ClassGamify roadmap for templates, activity creation, assignments, student attempts, results, and AI-assisted remixing.
roadmap_feedback_description: Tell us which template, worksheet, or review workflow would save the most preparation time for your class.
roadmap_feedback_title: Help choose the next classroom workflow
roadmap_principle_focus_description: We build around the Activity -> Assignment -> Attempt -> Results loop before adding parallel surfaces.
roadmap_principle_learning_description: The same structured content should render as games, worksheets, listening prompts, and printable follow-up.
roadmap_snapshot_expansion_description: AI should draft, remix, differentiate, and summarize inside the teacher-reviewed activity model.
roadmap_snapshot_live_description: Reusable activities, assignment links, student runners, and teacher result review are the product foundation.
roadmap_snapshot_loop_description: The next passes deepen worksheet-style delivery, listening prompts, drag interactions, and reteach summaries.
roadmap_subtitle: A practical view of what is already usable, what is being tightened now, and where AI-assisted classroom activity creation comes next.
roadmap_validation_description: We prioritize work that can be tested in the teacher flow, improves assignment delivery or review, and strengthens the data model instead of creating one-off demos.
roadmap_validation_item_business_descrip: Teachers should understand why a workflow saves prep, delivery, or reteach time before any paid plan boundary appears.
roadmap_validation_item_learning_descrip: A feature is stronger when the same activity can become a game, assignment, worksheet, and review artifact without losing context.
roadmap_validation_item_workflow_descrip: Classroom and learning-center workflows should handle sharing links, student names, result retention, and team permissions deliberately.
roadmap_validation_title: Every roadmap item needs classroom proof
settings_billing_card_current_plan_descr: Your ClassGamify plan, activity access, and hosted billing status
settings_billing_card_free_plan_message: Free keeps the starter activity workflow open. Upgrade when you need richer worksheets, saved classroom routines, larger activity libraries, and repeatable assignment handoffs.
settings_billing_card_lifetime_message: You have lifetime access to the current paid toolkit and early access to future classroom activity expansions as they ship.
settings_billing_card_no_plan: No paid plan is connected yet. You can keep using the free activity workflow, then choose a plan when reusable classroom assignments become useful.
settings_billing_card_upgrade_cta: Upgrade when you need custom worksheets, saved activity sets, and repeatable assignment workflows
settings_billing_description: Review your ClassGamify workspace access, hosted checkout status, and plan management options

settings_files_description: Manage classroom materials for future activity audio, worksheet images, and documents.
settings_files_upload_dialog_description: Store a classroom material under your account before attaching it to future activities.
settings_files_upload_error: Could not upload the file. Please check the file and try again.
settings_notification_description: Choose whether this teacher workspace receives ClassGamify product updates.
settings_notification_newsletter_descrip: Receive occasional updates about classroom templates, worksheet workflows, and assignment review features.
settings_notification_newsletter_email_r: Email is required to receive ClassGamify updates
settings_notification_newsletter_error: An error occurred while updating your subscription
settings_notification_newsletter_hint: You can change classroom update emails at any time.
settings_payment_failed_description: Please return to the ClassGamify pricing page and start checkout again.
settings_payment_processing_description: Please wait while we verify your ClassGamify plan access.
settings_payment_success_description: Redirecting you back to your ClassGamify workspace billing page.
settings_payment_timeout_description: You can close this page and check Billing for your classroom workspace access.
settings_profile_avatar_description: Upload a teacher avatar for your classroom workspace.
settings_profile_avatar_fail: Could not update your avatar. Please try again.
settings_profile_avatar_file_size_error: File size exceeds the classroom file storage limit.
settings_profile_avatar_hint: An avatar is optional, but it helps students recognize the teacher workspace.
settings_profile_avatar_upload_not_confi: Upload Avatar (classroom file storage not configured)
settings_profile_description: Set the teacher identity shown across your ClassGamify workspace.
settings_profile_name_description: Choose the display name used on your activities and assignments.
settings_profile_name_fail: Could not update your name. Please try again.
settings_profile_name_hint: Use 3-30 characters for the teacher display name
settings_security_delete_account_confirm: Delete this teacher account and its workspace data? This action cannot be undone.
settings_security_delete_account_descrip: Permanently remove this teacher account and saved workspace data
settings_security_delete_account_fail: Could not delete your account. Please try again or contact support.
settings_security_delete_account_warning: Export any classroom records you need before continuing; this action is not reversible
settings_security_description: Control sign-in, password access, and account deletion for this teacher workspace.
settings_security_reset_password_descrip: Set an email password for this teacher account
settings_security_reset_password_info: Resetting your password lets you sign in to ClassGamify with email and password in addition to any connected social login. You will receive an email with reset instructions.
settings_security_update_password_descri: Change the password used for email sign-in to your teacher workspace
settings_security_update_password_fail: Could not update your password. Please check your current password and try again.
settings_security_update_password_hint: Use at least 8 characters for the workspace password
settings_security_update_password_new_mi: Password must be at least 8 characters
settings_security_user_accounts_fetch_er: Could not load connected sign-in methods.
site_description: ClassGamify helps teachers create game-based classroom activities, publish assignment links, and review student results.
student_runner_anonymous_attempt_descrip: This assignment does not collect student names. This browser will submit as {label}.
student_runner_missing_assignment_closed: This assignment link has been closed by the teacher. Ask your teacher for a reopened or new link.
student_runner_missing_assignment_descri: This link may have been unpublished, closed, or typed incorrectly.
student_runner_missing_assignment_draft_: This assignment has not been published for students yet. Ask your teacher to share the published link.
student_runner_missing_assignment_expire: This assignment link has expired. Students can no longer open the activity from this link.
student_runner_public_assignment_descrip: This public assignment loads from the teacher share link, collects answers, and scores against the teacher's frozen assignment snapshot.
student_runner_read_only_preview: Preview assignments are read-only until a teacher publishes a share link.
student_runner_seo_description: Open a public student activity runner from a teacher assignment link.
student_runner_time_expired: Time is up. Review your saved answers, then submit.
teachers_page_audience_type: Teachers, tutors, schools, and learning centers
teachers_page_description: ClassGamify supports the real Wordwall-style loop: create an activity, switch templates, publish a share link, and review student results.
teachers_page_school_cta_description: Multi-teacher use needs thoughtful decisions about student names, result retention, template sharing, and classroom permissions.
teachers_page_school_cta_title: Need a school or learning-center workflow?
teachers_page_seo_description: Plan game-based classroom activities, publish assignment links, and collect student results with ClassGamify.
teachers_page_title: Build repeatable game-based assignments from the lessons you already teach.
teachers_page_use_case_0_description: Assign short games after class and see which students completed the activity.
teachers_page_use_case_1_description: Use box reveals, listening, line matching, sorting, and matching activities for warmups, review rounds, or small groups.
teachers_page_use_case_2_description: Use completions and scores to decide which items need reteaching or another activity.
teachers_page_workflow_0_description: Paste vocabulary, questions, examples, or worksheet prompts into one structured activity model.
teachers_page_workflow_1_description: Render the same content as quiz, matching games, line matching, group sorting, fill-ins, listening, pair matching, or box reveal play.
teachers_page_workflow_2_description: Share a public student link while keeping the reusable activity in the teacher library.
templates_page_bottom_description: Open the editor, load an example for the selected game format, then publish it as a shareable student assignment link.
templates_page_description: ClassGamify templates render shared questions, pairs, groups, and vocabulary as quick checks, matching games, worksheet practice, listening prompts, or whole-class reveal rounds.
templates_page_seo_description: Browse the first ClassGamify activity templates for quiz, matching games, line matching, group sorting, fill-ins, listening, pair matching, and box reveal classroom play.
templates_page_title: Pick a game format for the same lesson content.
user_files_api_error_content_type_mismat: The file extension does not match the uploaded content. Choose the original file or export it again.
user_files_api_error_dangerous_content_t: This file type cannot be uploaded for classroom safety.
user_files_api_error_file_too_large: The file is too large. Choose a file up to {maxMegabytes} MB.
user_files_api_error_invalid_file_type: This file type is not supported. Supported types: {supportedExtensions}.
user_files_api_error_storage_unavailable: File storage is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
user_files_api_error_upload_failed: Something went wrong while uploading the file
worksheets_page_description: ClassGamify treats fill-in practice, line matching, listening, and classification as playable assignment templates. Teachers create reusable content once, publish a student link, and review results without exposing answer keys before submission.
worksheets_page_mode_fill_blank_descript: Place short answers directly into sentence gaps for grammar, spelling, vocabulary, or reading checks.
worksheets_page_mode_group_sort_descript: Ask learners to classify words, examples, or concepts into teacher-defined groups before seeing the answer pattern.
worksheets_page_mode_line_match_descript: Turn terms and definitions into a two-column connection board that feels familiar to worksheet users.
worksheets_page_mode_listening_descripti: Use spoken tracks for dictation, comprehension, or pronunciation follow-up while hiding transcripts before review.
worksheets_page_printable_description: The first product pass focuses on interactive worksheets with scoring, attempts, accepted answers, and result exports. Printable practice and teacher-uploaded worksheet extraction should extend the same activity snapshot and results model instead of creating a separate worksheet product.
worksheets_page_printable_title: Printable follow-up can build on the same assignment record.
worksheets_page_seo_description: Create worksheet-style classroom activities with fill blanks, line matching, listening prompts, group sorting, assignment links, and teacher results.
worksheets_page_templates_cta_descriptio: Browse the full template library to switch the same structured content into quiz, matching games, box reveals, and group play.
worksheets_page_templates_cta_title: Want a different game view for the same lesson?
worksheets_page_title: Worksheet modes for the same activity content.
worksheets_page_workflow_step_1: Paste lesson material once into questions, pairs, groups, vocabulary, and notes.
worksheets_page_workflow_step_2: Choose a worksheet-style template and review the example before saving.
worksheets_page_workflow_step_3: Publish a student assignment link with attempts, timer, answer reveal, and close time.
worksheets_page_workflow_step_4: Review submissions, accepted answers, reteach priorities, and CSV exports.
